page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 2835 GB of local NVMe storage and supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10% via stress-ng, while multi-core performance falls within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance for cached small-block reads and large-block writes, though uncached large-block read bandwidth is average. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, perform in the top 10% tier.

Lacking a dedicated GPU, this server is not configured for heavy graphics processing or massive parallel AI training, but it demonstrates top-tier efficiency for small-scale LLM inference. The combination of 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage makes this configuration highly cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads. It is optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 192.0 GB of RAM and 2835 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression in the top 10%, while multi-core operations and database performance rank in the top 25%. A tradeoff is its average uncached large-block memory read bandwidth and the absence of a dedicated GPU. This server is optimized for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v7 features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server combining high-speed AMD EPYC processing, substantial local storage, and strong database execution capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of local NVMe storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong database operations and Redis caching capabilities
Optimized for general-purpose enterprise applications and static web serving
